completed.name: combine-dunning-workers
completed.description: Combine DunningSweepWorker and DunningAttemptWorker into a single worker
steps: Create single DunningWorker that runs periodically (e.g., every few hours)
Query all past_due subscriptions where next_retry_at is in the past
Loop through each subscription and attempt rebill via NMI
Update database after each attempt for idempotency (in case of crash)
On success: renew subscription, create payment record, reset retry counter
On failure: increment retry count, schedule next retry, or mark failed if max retries
Remove old DunningSweepWorker and DunningAttemptWorker
Update River job registration

completed.name: test-dunning-worker

Run DunningWorker
Verify subscriptions renewed on success, or failed on decline
Verify payment records created for successful rebills
Verify subscriptions not yet due are not processed

completed.name: stripe-like-api-structs
completed.description: Create Stripe-like response envelopes (Object wrappers) with our pagination system
steps: Create `pkg/api/response.go` defining standard response structs.
Define `ListResponse` struct with `object: list`, `data`, `total_items`, `page`, `page_size`, `total_pages` fields (NOT Stripe's has_more - we prefer explicit pagination).
Define wrappers for core resources: `ProductObject`, `PriceObject`, `SubscriptionObject`.
Define `PaymentIntentObject` and `NextActionObject` for handling redirects.
Update existing handlers to use these wrappers for JSON responses (starting with `GetProducts`).
Verify tests still pass or update them to expect new JSON structure.

completed.name: stripe-like-api-subscriptions
completed.description: Refactor Subscription creation endpoints to cleaner RESTful patterns
steps: Change `POST /v1/subscriptions/process/:processor` to `POST /v1/subscriptions/:provider` (e.g., /mobius, /ccbill, /solana).
Register explicit routes for each provider rather than using a dynamic :provider param to avoid collision with :id.
Move `POST /v1/subscriptions/ccbill/flexform-url` to `POST /v1/subscriptions/ccbill` (same endpoint, just generates FlexForm URL).
Simplify webhooks: `POST /v1/webhooks/:provider` instead of `/v1/subscriptions/webhook/:processor/:provider` (e.g., /webhooks/mobius, /webhooks/ccbill).
Update tests to use new endpoint paths.

Add SetTime() or Advance() methods to test clock for controlling time in tests

Test dunning/retry logic with time advancement

completed.name: fix-test-cancel-access-at-period-end
completed.description: Fix TestCancelAccessAtPeriodEnd to properly test period-end revocation with mock clock
steps: Current test uses indefinite entitlement (EndAt: nil) which never expires regardless of time

If using period-end worker: inject mock clock into worker, run worker after advancing clock past period end
If setting EndAt: set entitlement EndAt = subscription period end, then verify IsEntitled returns false after advancing clock
Ensure test actually verifies entitlements are revoked at period end, not just that subscription is cancelled

completed.name: fix-test-dunning-retry-schedule

Run DunningWorker when clock is before next_retry_at - verify NO rebill attempt made
Advance clock past next_retry_at
Run DunningWorker again - verify rebill attempt IS made
This requires DunningWorker to use injected clock for time comparisons

completed.name: webhook-lifecycle-entitlement-date-bug
completed.description: Webhook lifecycle tests fail due to PostgreSQL tstzrange constraint when entitlement start_at equals end_at
steps: [DONE] PROBLEM: PostgreSQL tstzrange(start_at, end_at, '[)') requires start_at < end_at, throws 'range lower bound must be less than or equal to range upper bound'
[DONE] ROOT CAUSE: Tests created entitlements at real time, but mock clock was set to past, causing end_at < start_at
[DONE] FIX 1: Updated entitlement service/repo to use injected mock clock instead of time.Now()
[DONE] FIX 2: Updated SubscriptionLifecycleService to propagate clock to EntitlementService instances
[DONE] FIX 3: Updated entitlement repo methods (EndActiveBySubscription, EndActiveByPayment, RevokeByID) to accept 'now' parameter
[DONE] FIX 4: Updated test helpers (CreateTestEntitlement, CreateTestSubscriptionWithOptions) to use suite.GetClock().Now()
[DONE] FIX 5: Updated webhook lifecycle tests to set mock clock BEFORE creating test data, then advance clock before revocation
[DONE] Added validation in entitlement Insert() to reject entitlements with end_at <= start_at
[DONE] Added validation in EndActiveBySubscription/Payment to error if revocation would create invalid date ranges
All affected tests now pass:
- TestWebhookSubscriptionLifecycle/Cancellation_cancels_subscription
- TestWebhookExpirationFlow/Expiration_cancels_subscription_and_revokes_entitlements
- TestWebhookChargebackTerminatesSubscription

completed.name: simplify-solana-pay-architecture
completed.description: Simplify Solana Pay to use Redis for pending state, server-side polling, and eliminate complex state machine

summary.overview: Replaced complex SolanaPaymentIntent state machine with simpler architecture: Redis for ephemeral pending payments, background goroutine polling Solana every 500ms, Postgres only for confirmed payments.

endpoints.POST /v1/solana/pay: Generate Solana Pay Transfer Request URL, store in Redis
endpoints.GET /v1/solana/pay/:reference: Alternative status check by path param

redis_structure.pending_solana_payments: SET of reference strings currently being polled

summary.poller: SolanaPayPoller goroutine with 500ms ticker, batch RPC queries
summary.latency: ~500-900ms from user signing tx to confirmed in our system

steps: [DONE] Create SolanaPayService with Redis storage
[DONE] Create SolanaPayPoller goroutine with 500ms polling
[DONE] Create POST /v1/solana/pay handler
[DONE] Create GET /v1/solana/pay/status handler
[DONE] Create GET /v1/solana/pay/:reference handler
[DONE] Register routes in routes_public.go
[DONE] Integration with CheckoutService.RegisterPurchase() for entitlements
completed.completed_notes: Implemented simplified Solana Pay architecture with Redis for pending payments, 500ms server-side polling, and Postgres only for confirmed payments. Routes registered in routes_public.go. Remaining cleanup (removing old SolanaPaymentIntent code) can be done separately.

completed.completed_notes: Added normalizeCurrency() helper function that extracts currency code from webhook data and normalizes to lowercase. Updated 12 locations from hardcoded 'usd' to use actual webhook currency fields. Uses BilledCurrencyCode for success events and AccountingCurrencyCode for refund/void/chargeback events. RenewalFailure event doesn't include currency in CCBill webhook format so it defaults to 'usd' with a comment explaining why.

This provides an audit trail for fraud monitoring and dispute resolution. RenewalSuccess has fewer card fields available from CCBill (no Bin, AVS, CVV2, 3DS).

completed.name: simplify-webhook-processing
completed.description: Remove async webhook retry mechanism - process webhooks synchronously only

context.problem: Async retry adds complexity without clear benefit. If webhook fails, CCBill/NMI will retry from their end.
context.solution: Process webhooks synchronously, store to DB for audit logging, rely on payment processors to retry

files_modified: internal/river/jobs_webhooks.go - removed WebhookProcessWorker and WebhookRetryWorker
internal/app/river_register.go - removed webhook worker registration and periodic job
internal/services/webhook_event_service.go - simplified to audit logging only (removed retry logic)
internal/services/webhook_processor.go - simplified to use Get() instead of BeginProcessing()
config/config.go - deprecated WebhookRetryConfig (kept for backwards compatibility)
tests/river_worker_test.go - removed webhook retry job tests
steps: [DONE] Remove WebhookRetryWorker from River jobs
[DONE] Remove WebhookProcessWorker from River jobs
[DONE] Remove webhook worker registration from river_register.go
[DONE] Remove webhook retry periodic job from buildRiverPeriodicJobs()
[DONE] Keep WebhookEventService for audit logging (simplified - removed retry methods)
[DONE] Remove ListRetryable(), BeginProcessing(), nextBackoff() methods
[DONE] Rename MarkFailure() to MarkFailed() (simpler signature)
[DONE] Deprecate WebhookRetryConfig in config.go
[DONE] Update tests to remove retry-related test cases
completed.completed_notes: Webhook processing is now synchronous-only. Webhooks are stored to DB for audit logging, processed immediately, and marked as processed/failed. No background retry workers. Payment processors (CCBill, NMI) will retry failed webhooks from their end.

completed.name: normalize-currency-lowercase

steps: [DONE] Change default currency from 'USD' to 'usd' throughout codebase

[DONE] CCBill webhook handlers use normalizeCurrency() to lowercase incoming currency

[DONE] API responses always return lowercase currency
[N/A] Migration seed data - no seed data in migrations

completed.completed_notes: All currency values are now normalized to lowercase throughout the codebase. Webhook handlers (CCBill and NMI) normalize incoming uppercase currencies from payment processors to lowercase before storing. Seed data in tests/seed_data.go uses lowercase. API documentation updated to reflect lowercase currency codes. NMI integration tests send uppercase 'USD' to NMI API (which is correct - NMI expects uppercase), but our internal storage uses lowercase.

completed.name: standardize-pagination-format
completed.description: Standardize all list endpoints to use Stripe-like pagination: object/data/total/limit/offset/has_more
completed.completed_notes: All list endpoints now use Stripe-like pagination format with object:'list', data, total, limit, offset, has_more. Two helper methods exist: api.NewListResponse (generic typed) and SuccessJSONPaginated (for dynamic data).

completed.name: prorated-upgrades-downgrades
completed.description: Implement prorated upgrades and scheduled downgrades for subscription tier changes
features_implemented: Tier group detection via Product.TierGroup and Product.TierRank
Upgrade detection: user on lower tier trying to purchase higher tier
Downgrade detection: user on higher tier trying to purchase lower tier
Scheduled downgrades via Subscription.ScheduledPriceID - applied at renewal
Entitlement granting on upgrade (new entitlements from higher tier)
Entitlement revocation on downgrade (remove entitlements not in lower tier)
files_modified: internal/services/lifecycle_service.go - RenewMembership() now applies scheduled downgrades
internal/services/webhook_ccbill.go - updateEntitlementsForUpgrade() now uses RevokeBySubscriptionAndName
internal/db/models/subscription.go - ActivateWithPrice() now updates ProductID
internal/db/models/entitlement.go - Added EntitlementRevokeDowngrade, EntitlementRevokeChargeback constants
internal/services/entitlement_service.go - Added RevokeBySubscriptionAndName()
internal/db/repo/entitlement.go - Added RevokeBySubscriptionAndName()
completed.completed_notes: Full upgrade/downgrade system is implemented. Upgrades are immediate with proration charge. Downgrades are scheduled and applied at next renewal via RenewMembership(). Entitlements are correctly granted/revoked based on product tier differences. Fixed bug where ActivateWithPrice() wasn't updating ProductID, and fixed bug where updateEntitlementsForUpgrade() was revoking ALL entitlements instead of just specific ones.

completed.name: merge-manage-subscription-service
completed.description: Merge ManageSubscriptionService into AdminSubscriptionService - it's redundant technical debt
files_modified: internal/services/admin_subscription_service.go - Added UpdateStatusParams struct and UpdateStatus method
internal/services/admin_subscription_service.go - Added ExtendSubscriptionByDuration method
internal/handlers/manage.go - Updated to use AdminSubscriptionService instead of ManageSubscriptionService
internal/services/lifecycle_service.go - Removed unused variables from FailMembership
changes: Added UpdateStatusParams struct with SubscriptionID, Status, CancelFeedback, CancelType fields
Added UpdateStatus() method to AdminSubscriptionService that handles status changes with validation
Added ExtendSubscriptionByDuration() method that ExtendSubscription() now calls internally
Updated manage.go handlers to use r.State.AdminSubscriptionService instead of creating ManageSubscriptionService
ManageSubscriptionService was never stored in runtime - handlers created it on-the-fly, now removed entirely
completed.completed_notes: Consolidated ManageSubscriptionService into AdminSubscriptionService. The manage.go handlers now use AdminSubscriptionService directly. This eliminates redundant code and simplifies the service layer. Also fixed pre-existing unused variable warnings in lifecycle_service.go that were blocking the build.

completed.name: update-subscription-payment-method
completed.description: Allow users to change which stored payment method their NMI subscription uses
files_modified: internal/integrations/nmi/nmi.go - Added UpdateSubscriptionPaymentSource(subscriptionID, customerVaultID)
internal/handlers/requests.go - Added UpdateSubscriptionPaymentMethodBodyParams and request type
internal/server/routes_public.go - Added PUT /v1/me/subscriptions/payment-method route
tests/subscribe_endpoint_test.go - Added update_subscription handling to MockNMIServer
tests/seed_data.go - Added GetSubscriptionByID() and SetPaymentMethodInactive() helpers
validations: User must own the subscription (from JWT)
User must own the target payment method
Subscription must be active or past_due (not cancelled)
completed.completed_notes: Implemented PUT /v1/me/subscriptions/payment-method endpoint allowing users to swap the payment method (customer vault) used for their NMI subscription. Particularly useful for users with past_due subscriptions who need to update their card after a failed payment. Handler validates all ownership and state requirements, calls NMI API to update the subscription's customer_vault_id, then updates local DB. Comprehensive test coverage for all validation scenarios and error cases.

completed.name: simplify-solana-payment-flow
completed.description: Simplified Solana payment flow - removed wallet linking/verification, payment intents, replaced with direct Solana Pay Transfer Request

context.problem: The Solana payment system was over-engineered with wallet verification challenges, payment intents, and complex state machines
context.solution: Simplified to 3 endpoints: list tokens, create payment URL, check status. No wallet linking required.

changes_made: Removed all wallet/payment-intent endpoints from routes
Payment state stored in Redis with 15-minute TTL (no database table needed)
solana_transactions table retained to link payments to on-chain transactions
new_flow: 1. POST /v1/solana/pay {price_id, token} → returns solana: URL + reference key
2. User scans QR/clicks URL, wallet creates and signs transaction
4. Backend verifies transaction on-chain, creates payment + entitlements
5. Status returns 'confirmed' with payment_id and signature

implementation_notes.ttl: 15-minute TTL on Redis pending payments - our business logic, not in Solana Pay spec
implementation_notes.polling: Server-side poller checks every 500ms for confirmed transactions
implementation_notes.official_sdk_comparison: Official @solana/pay SDK examples poll forever with no timeout - our TTL is better practice

completed.completed_notes: All 9 Solana tests pass. Flow is now much simpler - no wallet linking, no verification challenges, no payment intents. Just create payment URL → poll status → done.

completed.name: remove-wallet-verification-requirement
completed.description: Removed mandatory wallet verification before Solana payments - transaction signatures prove ownership

context.problem: Users had to link+verify wallet via challenge/sign before making a payment - unnecessary friction
context.solution: Completely removed wallet verification. Transaction signature itself proves wallet ownership.

changes_made: POST /v1/solana/pay accepts any price_id + token, no wallet pre-registration needed
User's wallet is implicit in the transaction they sign
completed.completed_notes: Completed as part of simplify-solana-payment-flow. Users can now pay immediately without linking wallet first. The wallet address comes from the transaction the user signs, not from a pre-registered wallet record.
